codeReview.summary: Implementation correctly fixes loaded_entries() to return exactly one canonical entry per key path using upsert semantics in both merge_value() and load_env_overrides(). All VAL-SESS-007 scenarios are covered by 6 integration tests. Code is clean, well-documented, and follows project conventions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The upsert pattern for audit trail deduplication (when higher-priority layer overrides a key, update the existing entry rather than push a new one) is an important pattern not documented in AGENTS.md or anywhere in shared state. Future developers working on config features may not discover this pattern without reading the implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: crates/swell-core/src/config.rs:254-269 (merge_value upsert) and crates/swell-core/src/config.rs:219-239 (load_env_overrides upsert). Both functions now update existing entries in-place rather than pushing duplicates.

summary: Pass. The config-audit-trail feature is correctly implemented. The key fix was changing merge_value() and load_env_overrides() from push-based (always appending ConfigEntry) to upsert-based (updating existing entry when a higher-priority layer provides the same key). This ensures loaded_entries() returns exactly one canonical entry per key path, traceable to the winning source file. 6 integration tests cover all VAL-SESS-007 scenarios. All 209 existing unit tests continue to pass, plus 6 new audit trail tests, 12 config_section_merge tests, and 1 doc-test. Clippy clean. No blocking issues.
